text.title: go-backend Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-backend as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Defining interfaces in the impl package (repository defines UserRepository; service uses it). This creates an import cycle: service imports repository, and if repository defines the interface, service must also import repository to import the interface.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Circular imports are forbidden in Go. The import cycle breaks the build. The correct pattern is: service defines the interface, repository implements it. This makes service depend on nothing except its own interfaces, and repository depends on service only for the interface it needs to implement.

failure-mode.description: Capturing gin.Context or c in a goroutine: go func() { h.userService.Process(c) }(). Then inside Process, using c.Request or c.Param — these are accessed from a different goroutine than the one that owns them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: gin.Context is request-scoped and not thread-safe. Accessing it from a goroutine is a data race. The correct pattern is: derive ctx := c.Request.Context() before spawning the goroutine, then pass ctx and the extracted values (e.g., ID from c.Param("id")) as arguments.

failure-mode.description: Calling c.JSON(...) directly in error cases: if err != nil { c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()}) return }. This bypasses error middleware.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Centralized error middleware (using c.Error(...) + ErrorHandler) ensures all errors are logged, mapped, and formatted consistently. If handlers call c.JSON directly, they sidestep the middleware, leading to inconsistent responses and missing logs.

failure-mode.description: Bloating main.go with business logic: parsing config, database setup, service initialization, and handler registration all in main. The file becomes 200+ lines.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Testing is impossible: main imports internal/*, sets up the database, and runs the server. A test cannot import main and run a subset of the logic. The correct pattern is: main only wires (config → deps → router → server), keeping business logic in handler/service/repository.
